This changes the `prompt!` design to not consume on the two tasks until inside the lock.

~However, with this design I find that there's a 50% chance that interactive startup with `-t2` will block typing while Pkg loads.~

~As an attempt to avoid typing blocking in this design, suggested by @vchuravy I've made `repl_main` run on a non-sticky task and changing any repl `@async` calls to `@spawn` to avoid thread-pinning the repl task.~

~But the 50% chance of blocking typing while Pkg loads remains.~

~It seems the repl task is still not freely switching threads.~

Spawning `prompt!` specifically avoids typing blocking, because the general repl task is made sticky by use of `_wait2` as well as the `@async`s.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/54785/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/54785/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/comments/2181053084","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/54785#issuecomment-2181053084","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/54785","id":2181053084,"node_id":"IC_kwDOABkWpM6CADqc","user":{"login":"nhz2","id":39104088,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjM5MTA0MDg4","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/39104088?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/nhz2","html_url":"https://github.com/nhz2","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nhz2/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nhz2/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nhz2/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nhz2/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nhz2/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nhz2/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nhz2/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nhz2/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nhz2/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2024-06-20T16:04:07Z","updated_at":"2024-06-20T16:04:07Z","author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","body":"I think the underlying issue is that the default `peek`:

https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/blob/0a491e00a1f38b814ca173bd7d9bffeadde65738/base/io.jl#L341-L347

removes the mark on the stream, by calling `reset`.

So if a function calls `peek` after marking the stream, that mark is unexpectedly lost.

`read(io, ::Char)` internally uses `peek(io, ::UInt8)` if reading non ascii:
https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/blob/0a491e00a1f38b814ca173bd7d9bffeadde65738/base/io.jl#L969

1. Download the repository
2. Follow instructions at https://github.com/Blosc/c-blosc?tab=readme-ov-file#compiling-the-blosc-library
3. Run `$ cd ../examples`
4. Run `$ gcc -O3 -msse2 simple.c -I../blosc -o simple -L../build/blosc -lblosc` Note the added `-lblosc` compared to the instructions in "simple.c"
5. Run `$ export LD_LIBRARY_PATH=../build/blosc`

After these steps I get the expected output:
```sh
$ ./simple
Blosc version info: 1.21.7.dev ($Date:: 2024-06-24 #$)
Compression: 4000000 -> 36321 (110.1x)
Decompression successful!
Successful roundtrip!
```","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/Blosc/c-blosc/issues/comments/2189457313/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}

{"action":"opened","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/54369","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/54369/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/54369/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/54369/events","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/issues/54369","id":2279780500,"node_id":"I_kwDOABkWpM6H4rCU","number":54369,"title":"`String(v::Memory{UInt8})` doesn't make a copy","user":{"login":"nhz2","id":39104088,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjM5MTA0MDg4","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/39104088?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/nhz2","html_url":"https://github.com/nhz2","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nhz2/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nhz2/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nhz2/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nhz2/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nhz2/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nhz2/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nhz2/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nhz2/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nhz2/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":0,"created_at":"2024-05-05T23:06:08Z","updated_at":"2024-05-05T23:06:08Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"<!--
If you have a question please search or post to our Discourse site: https://discourse.julialang.org.
We use the GitHub issue tracker for bug reports and feature requests only.

If you're submitting a bug report, be sure to include as much relevant information as
possible, including:

2. How you installed Julia
3. A minimal working example (MWE), also known as a minimum reproducible example

If you're experiencing a problem with a particular package, open an issue on that
package's repository instead.

Thanks for contributing to the Julia project!
-->
The docstring of `String` says:

> To avoid truncation
  of Vector{UInt8} data, use String(copy(v)); for other AbstractVector types,
  String(v) already makes a copy.

Since `Memory` is an `AbstractVector`, `String` should make a copy. However, it doesn't look like this is happening in:

https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/blob/9d8e0e7016ad64890f81b1fd93c1d46de9588a02/base/strings/string.jl#L67-L71

```julia
julia> v = Memory{UInt8}(undef, 10);

julia> fill!(v, 0x01);

julia> String(v)
"\x01\x01\x01\x01\x01\x01\x01\x01\x01\x01"

julia> v
0-element Memory{UInt8}
```

Maybe the current `String(v::Memory{UInt8})` method could be renamed to something like `take_string!` to make it clear that it can mutate `v`.

-function String(v::Memory{UInt8})
-    len = length(v)
-    len == 0 && return ""

-end
+String(v::AbstractVector{UInt8}) = take_string!(copyto!(StringMemory(length(v)), v))

```julia
julia> using BenchmarkTools

julia> function foo!(v::Memory{UInt8})
           len = length(v)
           len == 0 && return ""
           return ccall(:jl_genericmemory_to_string, Ref{String}, (Any, Int), v, len)
       end
foo! (generic function with 1 method)

julia> bar1(v) = foo!(copyto!(Base.StringMemory(length(v)), v))
bar1 (generic function with 1 method)

julia> bar2(v) = foo!(copy(v))
bar2 (generic function with 1 method)

julia> a = Base.StringMemory(1_000_000);

julia> fill!(a, 0x61);

julia> @btime bar1($a);
  34.581 μs (2 allocations: 976.72 KiB)

julia> @btime bar2($a);
  77.967 μs (3 allocations: 1.91 MiB)

I think it would be helpful to have a function.

```julia
copy_string(v::AbstractVector{UInt8}) = take_string!(copyto!(StringMemory(length(v)), v)))
```

I could use this to fix `ZipArchives.jl` for Julia 1.11 by replacing current uses of the `String` constructor with `copy_string` or `take_string!`.

Would this be better in a separate `CopyStrings.jl` package?","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/54424/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/54424/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null}}

calling `String` on a `view` of an `AbstractVector{UInt8}` is potential UB https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/54372#issuecomment-2102885169 
I think it would be helpful to have a function.

```julia
copystring(v::AbstractVector{UInt8}) = take_string!(copyto!(StringMemory(length(v)), v)))
```

I could use this to fix `ZipArchives.jl` by replacing current uses of the `String` constructor on vectors of bytes with `copystring`.

Would this be better in a separate `CopyStrings.jl` package?","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/54424/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/54424/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/comments/2104822357","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/issues/54424#issuecomment-2104822357","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/54424","id":2104822357,"node_id":"IC_kwDOABkWpM59dQpV","user":{"login":"nhz2","id":39104088,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjM5MTA0MDg4","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/39104088?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/nhz2","html_url":"https://github.com/nhz2","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nhz2/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nhz2/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nhz2/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nhz2/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nhz2/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nhz2/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nhz2/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nhz2/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nhz2/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2024-05-10T15:38:14Z","updated_at":"2024-05-10T15:38:14Z","author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","body":"Here is an example where using the `String` constructor on a `Vector{UInt8}` can result in mutating a `String`

```julia
io = IOBuffer()
write(io, [0x61,0x62,0x63])
seekstart(io)
a = read(io, 3)
b = reshape(a, 1, 3)
c = reshape(b, 3)
s = String(a)
@show s
c[1] = 0x62
@show s
```
This changes `s` from `"abc"` to `"bbc"`
This happens on Julia 1.6 as well as nightly.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/comments/2104822357/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}

{"action":"created","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/54447","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/54447/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/54447/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/54447/events","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/54447","id":2291418015,"node_id":"PR_kwDOABkWpM5vL0Y8","number":54447,"title":"Ensure `isdigit`","user":{"login":"Seelengrab","id":11753998,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjExNzUzOTk4","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/11753998?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/Seelengrab","html_url":"https://github.com/Seelengrab","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Seelengrab/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Seelengrab/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Seelengrab/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Seelengrab/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Seelengrab/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Seelengrab/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Seelengrab/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Seelengrab/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Seelengrab/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[{"id":82096350,"node_id":"MDU6TGFiZWw4MjA5NjM1MA==","url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/labels/domain:unicode","name":"domain:unicode","color":"5f4a60","default":false,"description":"Related to unicode characters and encodings"}],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":4,"created_at":"2024-05-12T17:00:11Z","updated_at":"2024-05-12T20:52:59Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","active_lock_reason":null,"draft":false,"pull_request":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/pulls/54447","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/54447","diff_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/54447.diff","patch_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/54447.patch","merged_at":null},"body":"I noticed that

```julia
julia> '４' |> isdigit
false
```

even though

```julia
julia> '４'
'４': Unicode U+FF14 (category Nd: Number, decimal digit)
```

The docstring doesn't say that this only checks ASCII, and the unicode category (see [here](https://www.unicode.org/reports/tr44/#Numeric_Type)) is an exact match for the definition (ten glyphs making up the numbers zero through nine, in various scripts). So while some things will still slip through (e.g. [`四`](https://jisho.org/word/%E5%9B%9B), which is considered a letter by Unicode), this implementation is a tiny bit more correct.

However, it's not just roses and sunshine; there is a downside, in that this won't vectorize anymore due to the `ccall` behind `category_code`. We could bring the double table lookup into pure Julia, but I'm not sure whether that would restore performance here. Maybe it could? As is, this PR comes with a 100x regression in a microbenchmark.

v1.11-alpha2:

```julia
julia> @benchmark count(isdigit, v) setup=(v=rand(Char, 10_000))
BenchmarkTools.Trial: 10000 samples with 202 evaluations.
 Range (min … max):  392.871 ns … 509.455 ns  ┊ GC (min … max): 0.00% … 0.00%
 Time  (median):     400.743 ns               ┊ GC (median):    0.00%
 Time  (mean ± σ):   405.332 ns ±  12.212 ns  ┊ GC (mean ± σ):  0.00% ± 0.00%

       ▂▅█▆▇▆▄▂                  ▁▁   ▁▃▂▂▂                     ▂
  ▆▂▅▅██████████▇▆▅▄▄▄▄▂▄▄▄▅▅▅▆▆▇██▇▇▆███████▇▇▇▆▇▆▆▇▆▆▆▇█████▇ █
  393 ns        Histogram: log(frequency) by time        446 ns <

 Memory estimate: 0 bytes, allocs estimate: 0.
```

This PR:

```julia
julia> @benchmark count(isdigit, v) setup=(v=rand(Char, 10_000))
BenchmarkTools.Trial: 10000 samples with 1 evaluation.
 Range (min … max):  40.870 μs … 483.089 μs  ┊ GC (min … max): 0.00% … 0.00%
 Time  (median):     42.200 μs               ┊ GC (median):    0.00%
 Time  (mean ± σ):   45.848 μs ±  19.561 μs  ┊ GC (mean ± σ):  0.00% ± 0.00%

  █▆▃▃▂                                                        ▁
  ████████▆▆▄▄▆▄▅▅▅▄▅▅▇▄▄▄▄▄▁▁▁▃▁▃▁▁▁▁▁▁▁▁▃▇██▇▅▆▃▆█▄▄▄▃▁▁▁▁▁█ █
  40.9 μs       Histogram: log(frequency) by time       129 μs <

 Memory estimate: 0 bytes, allocs estimate: 0.
```

In a single-invocation benchmark, we "merely" have a 2-3x/10ns regression:

v1.11-alpha2:

```julia
julia> @benchmark isdigit(v) setup=(v=rand(Char)) evals=1
BenchmarkTools.Trial: 10000 samples with 1 evaluation.
 Range (min … max):  10.000 ns … 40.000 ns  ┊ GC (min … max): 0.00% … 0.00%
 Time  (median):     20.000 ns              ┊ GC (median):    0.00%
 Time  (mean ± σ):   17.548 ns ±  4.491 ns  ┊ GC (mean ± σ):  0.00% ± 0.00%

                                                            █  
  ▇▁▁▁▁▁▁▁▁▁▁▁▁▁▁▁▁▁▁▁▁▁▁▁▁▁▁▁▁▁▁▁▁▁▁▁▁▁▁▁▁▁▁▁▁▁▁▁▁▁▁▁▁▁▁▁▁▁█ ▂
  10 ns           Histogram: frequency by time          20 ns <

 Memory estimate: 0 bytes, allocs estimate: 0.
```

This PR:

```julia
julia> @benchmark isdigit(v) setup=(v=rand(Char)) evals=1
BenchmarkTools.Trial: 10000 samples with 1 evaluation.
 Range (min … max):  20.000 ns … 520.000 ns  ┊ GC (min … max): 0.00% … 0.00%
 Time  (median):     30.000 ns               ┊ GC (median):    0.00%
 Time  (mean ± σ):   32.170 ns ±  31.893 ns  ┊ GC (mean ± σ):  0.00% ± 0.00%

  ▇  █  ▂                      ▂   ▂                           ▁
  █▁▁█▁▁█▁▁▇▁▁▁▅▁▁▄▁▁▁▁▁▁▁▁▁▆▁▁█▁▁▁█▁▁█▁▁▅▁▁▁▄▁▁▃▁▁▄▁▁▁▃▁▁▇▁▁▅ █
  20 ns         Histogram: log(frequency) by time       200 ns <

 Memory estimate: 0 bytes, allocs estimate: 0.
```

That being said, we already check `category_code` pretty much everywhere else, and this microbenchmark is extremely unlikely to be representative of an actual workload. encodings"}],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":6,"created_at":"2024-05-12T17:00:11Z","updated_at":"2024-05-12T23:19:23Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","active_lock_reason":null,"draft":false,"pull_request":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/pulls/54447","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/54447","diff_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/54447.diff","patch_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/54447.patch","merged_at":null},"body":"I noticed that

```julia
julia> '４' |> isdigit
false
```

even though

```julia
julia> '４'
'４': Unicode U+FF14 (category Nd: Number, decimal digit)
```

The docstring doesn't say that this only checks ASCII, and the unicode category (see [here](https://www.unicode.org/reports/tr44/#Numeric_Type)) is an exact match for the definition (ten glyphs making up the numbers zero through nine, in various scripts). So while some things will still slip through (e.g. [`四`](https://jisho.org/word/%E5%9B%9B), which is considered a letter by Unicode), this implementation is a tiny bit more correct.

However, it's not just roses and sunshine; there is a downside, in that this won't vectorize anymore due to the `ccall` behind `category_code`. We could bring the double table lookup into pure Julia, but I'm not sure whether that would restore performance here. Maybe it could? As is, this PR comes with a 100x regression in a microbenchmark.

v1.11-alpha2:

```julia
julia> @benchmark count(isdigit, v) setup=(v=rand(Char, 10_000))
BenchmarkTools.Trial: 10000 samples with 202 evaluations.
 Range (min … max):  392.871 ns … 509.455 ns  ┊ GC (min … max): 0.00% … 0.00%
 Time  (median):     400.743 ns               ┊ GC (median):    0.00%
 Time  (mean ± σ):   405.332 ns ±  12.212 ns  ┊ GC (mean ± σ):  0.00% ± 0.00%

       ▂▅█▆▇▆▄▂                  ▁▁   ▁▃▂▂▂                     ▂
  ▆▂▅▅██████████▇▆▅▄▄▄▄▂▄▄▄▅▅▅▆▆▇██▇▇▆███████▇▇▇▆▇▆▆▇▆▆▆▇█████▇ █
  393 ns        Histogram: log(frequency) by time        446 ns <

 Memory estimate: 0 bytes, allocs estimate: 0.
```

This PR:

```julia
julia> @benchmark count(isdigit, v) setup=(v=rand(Char, 10_000))
BenchmarkTools.Trial: 10000 samples with 1 evaluation.
 Range (min … max):  40.870 μs … 483.089 μs  ┊ GC (min … max): 0.00% … 0.00%
 Time  (median):     42.200 μs               ┊ GC (median):    0.00%
 Time  (mean ± σ):   45.848 μs ±  19.561 μs  ┊ GC (mean ± σ):  0.00% ± 0.00%

  █▆▃▃▂                                                        ▁
  ████████▆▆▄▄▆▄▅▅▅▄▅▅▇▄▄▄▄▄▁▁▁▃▁▃▁▁▁▁▁▁▁▁▃▇██▇▅▆▃▆█▄▄▄▃▁▁▁▁▁█ █
  40.9 μs       Histogram: log(frequency) by time       129 μs <

 Memory estimate: 0 bytes, allocs estimate: 0.
```

In a single-invocation benchmark, we "merely" have a 2-3x/10ns regression:

v1.11-alpha2:

```julia
julia> @benchmark isdigit(v) setup=(v=rand(Char)) evals=1
BenchmarkTools.Trial: 10000 samples with 1 evaluation.
 Range (min … max):  10.000 ns … 40.000 ns  ┊ GC (min … max): 0.00% … 0.00%
 Time  (median):     20.000 ns              ┊ GC (median):    0.00%
 Time  (mean ± σ):   17.548 ns ±  4.491 ns  ┊ GC (mean ± σ):  0.00% ± 0.00%

                                                            █  
  ▇▁▁▁▁▁▁▁▁▁▁▁▁▁▁▁▁▁▁▁▁▁▁▁▁▁▁▁▁▁▁▁▁▁▁▁▁▁▁▁▁▁▁▁▁▁▁▁▁▁▁▁▁▁▁▁▁▁█ ▂
  10 ns           Histogram: frequency by time          20 ns <

 Memory estimate: 0 bytes, allocs estimate: 0.
```

This PR:

```julia
julia> @benchmark isdigit(v) setup=(v=rand(Char)) evals=1
BenchmarkTools.Trial: 10000 samples with 1 evaluation.
 Range (min … max):  20.000 ns … 520.000 ns  ┊ GC (min … max): 0.00% … 0.00%
 Time  (median):     30.000 ns               ┊ GC (median):    0.00%
 Time  (mean ± σ):   32.170 ns ±  31.893 ns  ┊ GC (mean ± σ):  0.00% ± 0.00%

  ▇  █  ▂                      ▂   ▂                           ▁
  █▁▁█▁▁█▁▁▇▁▁▁▅▁▁▄▁▁▁▁▁▁▁▁▁▆▁▁█▁▁▁█▁▁█▁▁▅▁▁▁▄▁▁▃▁▁▄▁▁▁▃▁▁▇▁▁▅ █
  20 ns         Histogram: log(frequency) by time       200 ns <

 Memory estimate: 0 bytes, allocs estimate: 0.
```

That being said, we already check `category_code` pretty much everywhere else, and this microbenchmark is extremely unlikely to be representative of an actual workload. {"action":"created","comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/pulls/comments/1598549545","pull_request_review_id":2052832127,"id":1598549545,"node_id":"PRRC_kwDOABkWpM5fR-4p","diff_hunk":"@@ -489,6 +489,62 @@ function take!(io::IOBuffer)
     return data
 end
 
+"Internal method. Copies the data from the IOBuffer to a string, leaving the
+IOBuffer in an unusable (erroneous) state.
+This assumes the IOBuffer is writable and seekable"
+function unsafe_takestring!(io::IOBuffer)
+    off = io.offset
+    nbytes = io.size - off
+    iszero(nbytes) && return ""
+    mem = StringMemory(nbytes)
+    unsafe_copyto!(mem, 1, io.data, off+1, nbytes)
+    return unsafe_takestring(mem)
+end
+
+"""
+    takestring!(io::IOBuffer) -> String
+
+Return the content of `io` as a `String`, resetting the buffer to its initial
+state.
+This is preferred over calling `String(take!(io))` to create a string from
+an `IOBuffer`.
+
+# Examples
+```jldoctest
+julia> io = IOBuffer();
+
+julia> write(io, [0x61, 0x62, 0x63]);
+
+julia> s = takestring!(io)
+"abc"
+
+julia> isempty(take!(io)) # io is now empty
+true
+```
+"""
+function takestring!(io::IOBuffer)","path":"base/iobuffer.jl","commit_id":"f1313b86c42901c2eebb4155b0e4e2b4e7bd5a3a","original_commit_id":"f1313b86c42901c2eebb4155b0e4e2b4e7bd5a3a","user":{"login":"nhz2","id":39104088,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjM5MTA0MDg4","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/39104088?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/nhz2","html_url":"https://github.com/nhz2","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nhz2/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nhz2/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nhz2/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nhz2/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nhz2/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nhz2/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nhz2/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nhz2/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nhz2/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"body":"This is missing a method for `GenericIOBuffer`.

calling `String` on a `view` of an `AbstractVector{UInt8}` is potential UB https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/54372#issuecomment-2102885169 
I think it would be helpful to have a function.

```julia
copystring(v::AbstractVector{UInt8}) = take_string!(copyto!(StringMemory(length(v)), v)))
```

I could use this to fix `ZipArchives.jl` by replacing current uses of the `String` constructor on vectors of bytes with `copystring`.

{"action":"created","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/54581","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/54581/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/54581/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/54581/events","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/issues/54581","id":2317701689,"node_id":"I_kwDOABkWpM6KJVI5","number":54581,"title":"Add a concept of memory-backed contiguous collection in Base","user":{"login":"jakobnissen","id":23193950,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjIzMTkzOTUw","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/23193950?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/jakobnissen","html_url":"https://github.com/jakobnissen","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jakobnissen/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jakobnissen/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jakobnissen/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jakobnissen/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jakobnissen/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jakobnissen/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jakobnissen/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jakobnissen/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jakobnissen/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":7,"created_at":"2024-05-26T13:04:27Z","updated_at":"2024-05-29T15:33:09Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"I propose the creation of a new concrete (parametric) type in Base that corresponds to memory-backed contiguous arrays, and to implement Base methods that specialize on these properties in terms of this type.

Edit: I've made a proof-of-concept package here: https://github.com/jakobnissen/MemViews.jl

## Motivation
Various places in Base and elsewhere, Julia has methods that can operate on _any_ contiguous memory region.
Let's call this type of data a _dense array_.
Examples include:
* Search methods which work by ccalling `memchr`, as in `string/search.jl`
* CRC32c
* Various IO methods, e.g. `read!(::IO, A::AbstractArray)`, which calls to `unsafe_write`.

This is currently handled inconsistently in Base:
* `string/search.jl` uses the internal `ByteArray` union type to cover dense arrays of bytes
* CRC32c uses `Union{Array{UInt8},FastContiguousSubArray{UInt8,N,<:Array{UInt8}} where N}`
* `read` dispatches on `AbstractArray`, and checks of `isbitstype(eltype(A)) && _checkcontiguous(Bool, A)`.

There are several issues with this handling:

First, all the approaches above fail to cover some important types. I've attempted to address this ad-hoc in #47693, #53178 and #54579. However, this ad-hoc patching is deeply unsatisfying, and I'm certain I have missed several types. In fact, I'm starting to think it's literally impossible to cover all permutations of views, reinterpretarrays, codeunits, memory and vector using a `Union`.
The practical implication is that lots of function calls needlessly fail to hit the fast path, while at the same time, the code is harder to reason about because the handling of dense arrays is ad hoc.

The fact that this code is duplicated thrice in Base, and in all three places was lacking important methods, suggests to me that there is a need for a better (internal) API to write methods for dense arrays. That was also my experience when making those three PR's: "Surely, there must be a better way to do this". The main usability issue is that in lieu of any API to cover dense arrays, it's up to the implementer of every method to make sure they've covered non-obvious types like `CodeUnits{UInt8, SubString{String}}` and `SubArray{UInt8, 1, Vector{UInt8}, Tuple{Base.OneTo{Int64}}, true}`. Unsurprisingly, people don't correctly do this.

There are also some other, minor issues with the existing approaches: Namely, it causes unnecessary specialization, as there is no reason to compile two methods for e.g. `String` and `Vector{UInt8}` if they each just operate on a slice of bytes in memory. Also, it's more difficult to introspect and reason about methods whose signature includes a "big union".

#### Why not use `DenseArray`?
Because it doesn't cover the correct types. Currently, it doesn't cover `SubArray`, despite many subarrays being dense vectors. Also, currently, we incorrectly have `CodeUnits <: DenseVector` (#53996). If this is fixed, then e.g. `CodeUnits{T, String}` will also not be covered by `DenseArray`.
Also, reinterpret may create dense arrays that are not `DenseArray`s.

## Proposal
My proposal here is slightly underspecified around the details - if people agree that the idea is good, I can cook up a proof of concept in a package. Ultimately, this will need to be in Base (probably as an internal type).

I propose creating two new, internal Base types with the following layout:
```julia
struct MemoryView{T} <: DenseVector{T}
    ref::MemoryRef{T}
    len::Int
end

struct MutableMemoryView{T} <: DenseVector{T}
    ref::MemoryRef{T}
    len::Int
end
```

These types can be constructed from the various contiguous, dense arrays. For example, you could have:
```julia
try_to_memory(x::Any) = nothing

function try_to_memory(s::Union{String, SubString{String}})
    MemoryView{UInt8}(MemoryRef(unsafe_wrap(Memory{UInt8}, s), ncodeunits(s))
end
```
Optimised methods can be implemented in terms of `MemoryView`, when it's not possible to write optimised methods generic to `AbstractArray`, and where `Union{Vector, Memory}` etc. is too restrictive:
```julia
function findnext(::typeof(iszero), mem::MemoryView{<:Union{UInt8, Int8}}, i::UInt)
    # the optimised function here, maybe calling ccall or intrinsics
end
```
Which would only need to be compiled once, and which is conceptually easier to understand then working with a huge union.

The surface level API would be along the lines of
```julia
function findnext(::typeof(iszero), mem, i::Integer)
    maybe_mem = try_to_memory(mem)
    if maybe_mem === nothing
        # generic implementation
    else
        findnext(iszero, maybe_mem, UInt(i)::UInt)
    end
end
```

## Alternatives
1. Instead of creating concrete types, the different parts in Base could more consistently use `Base._checkcontiguous` and `isbitstype`, perhaps extracted into a function `is_memory_backed_contiguous`. Base could then be reviewed for places where memory-backed contiguous types are used, and methods could be added to "funnel" all the compatible types into these methods.
Importantly, _any alternative approach should include an internal API_, such that it makes it easy to write a method that will correctly dispatch if, and only if, the argument is a dense array_ - which is _not_ the status quo.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/54581/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/54581/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/comments/2137705222","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/issues/54581#issuecomment-2137705222","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/54581","id":2137705222,"node_id":"IC_kwDOABkWpM5_assG","user":{"login":"nhz2","id":39104088,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjM5MTA0MDg4","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/39104088?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/nhz2","html_url":"https://github.com/nhz2","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nhz2/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nhz2/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nhz2/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nhz2/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nhz2/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nhz2/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nhz2/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nhz2/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nhz2/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2024-05-29T15:33:08Z","updated_at":"2024-05-29T15:33:08Z","author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","body":"Why is the use of `unsafe_wrap` in the following safe from GC?https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/blob/4b8fde32b428b467933986989f70f04537eeb438/base/iobuffer.jl#L44
It seems very similar to https://github.com/jakobnissen/MemViews.jl/blob/0fbcf578a85a9f370d8a2fdc64a46fedacea83be/src/construction.jl#L10-L12","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/comments/2137705222/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}

Various fixes to string searching

## Larger things that needs decisions
* The internal union `Base.ByteArray` has been deleted. Instead, the unions `DenseInt8` and `DenseUInt8` have been added. These more comprehensively cover the types that was meant, e.g. `Memory{UInt8}` was incorrectly not covered by the former. As stated in the TODO, the concept of a "memory backed dense byte array" is needed throughout Julia, so this ideally needs to be implemented as a single type and used throughout Base. The fix here is a decent temporary solution. See #53178 #54581
* The `findall` docstring between two arrays was incorrectly not attached to the method - now it is. **Note that this change _changes_ the documentation** since it includes a docstring that was previously missed. Hence, it's an API addition.

## Trivial fixes
* `findfirst(==(Int8(-1)), [0xff])` and similar findlast, findnext and findprev is no longer buggy, see #54578
* `findfirst([0x0ff], Int8[-1])` is similarly no longer buggy, see #54578
* `findnext(==('\xa6'), "æ", 1)` and `findprev(==('\xa6'), "æa", 2)` no longer incorrectly throws an error
* Using `Base.first_utf8_byte` instead of `x::AbstractChar % UInt8` is safer and faster
* Fixed incorrect use of `GC.@preserve`, where the pointer was taken before the preserve block.
* More of the optimised string methods now also apply to `SubString{String}`

Various fixes to string searching

## Larger things that needs decisions
* The internal union `Base.ByteArray` has been deleted. Instead, the unions `DenseInt8` and `DenseUInt8` have been added. These more comprehensively cover the types that was meant, e.g. `Memory{UInt8}` was incorrectly not covered by the former. As stated in the TODO, the concept of a "memory backed dense byte array" is needed throughout Julia, so this ideally needs to be implemented as a single type and used throughout Base. The fix here is a decent temporary solution. See #53178 #54581
* The `findall` docstring between two arrays was incorrectly not attached to the method - now it is. **Note that this change _changes_ the documentation** since it includes a docstring that was previously missed. Hence, it's an API addition.

## Trivial fixes
* `findfirst(==(Int8(-1)), [0xff])` and similar findlast, findnext and findprev is no longer buggy, see #54578
* `findfirst([0x0ff], Int8[-1])` is similarly no longer buggy, see #54578
* `findnext(==('\xa6'), "æ", 1)` and `findprev(==('\xa6'), "æa", 2)` no longer incorrectly throws an error
* Using `Base.first_utf8_byte` instead of `x::AbstractChar % UInt8` is safer and faster
* Fixed incorrect use of `GC.@preserve`, where the pointer was taken before the preserve block.
* More of the optimised string methods now also apply to `SubString{String}`

Various fixes to string searching

## Larger things that needs decisions
* The internal union `Base.ByteArray` has been deleted. Instead, the unions `DenseInt8` and `DenseUInt8` have been added. These more comprehensively cover the types that was meant, e.g. `Memory{UInt8}` was incorrectly not covered by the former. As stated in the TODO, the concept of a "memory backed dense byte array" is needed throughout Julia, so this ideally needs to be implemented as a single type and used throughout Base. The fix here is a decent temporary solution. See #53178 #54581
* The `findall` docstring between two arrays was incorrectly not attached to the method - now it is. **Note that this change _changes_ the documentation** since it includes a docstring that was previously missed. Hence, it's an API addition.

## Trivial fixes
* `findfirst(==(Int8(-1)), [0xff])` and similar findlast, findnext and findprev is no longer buggy, see #54578
* `findfirst([0x0ff], Int8[-1])` is similarly no longer buggy, see #54578
* `findnext(==('\xa6'), "æ", 1)` and `findprev(==('\xa6'), "æa", 2)` no longer incorrectly throws an error
* Using `Base.first_utf8_byte` instead of `x::AbstractChar % UInt8` is safer and faster
* Fixed incorrect use of `GC.@preserve`, where the pointer was taken before the preserve block.
* More of the optimised string methods now also apply to `SubString{String}`

Various fixes to string searching

## Larger things that needs decisions
* The internal union `Base.ByteArray` has been deleted. Instead, the unions `DenseInt8` and `DenseUInt8` have been added. These more comprehensively cover the types that was meant, e.g. `Memory{UInt8}` was incorrectly not covered by the former. As stated in the TODO, the concept of a "memory backed dense byte array" is needed throughout Julia, so this ideally needs to be implemented as a single type and used throughout Base. The fix here is a decent temporary solution. See #53178 #54581
* The `findall` docstring between two arrays was incorrectly not attached to the method - now it is. **Note that this change _changes_ the documentation** since it includes a docstring that was previously missed. Hence, it's an API addition.

## Trivial fixes
* `findfirst(==(Int8(-1)), [0xff])` and similar findlast, findnext and findprev is no longer buggy, see #54578
* `findfirst([0x0ff], Int8[-1])` is similarly no longer buggy, see #54578
* `findnext(==('\xa6'), "æ", 1)` and `findprev(==('\xa6'), "æa", 2)` no longer incorrectly throws an error
* Using `Base.first_utf8_byte` instead of `x::AbstractChar % UInt8` is safer and faster
* Fixed incorrect use of `GC.@preserve`, where the pointer was taken before the preserve block.
* More of the optimised string methods now also apply to `SubString{String}`

Various fixes to string searching

## Larger things that needs decisions
* The internal union `Base.ByteArray` has been deleted. Instead, the unions `DenseInt8` and `DenseUInt8` have been added. These more comprehensively cover the types that was meant, e.g. `Memory{UInt8}` was incorrectly not covered by the former. As stated in the TODO, the concept of a "memory backed dense byte array" is needed throughout Julia, so this ideally needs to be implemented as a single type and used throughout Base. The fix here is a decent temporary solution. See #53178 #54581
* The `findall` docstring between two arrays was incorrectly not attached to the method - now it is. **Note that this change _changes_ the documentation** since it includes a docstring that was previously missed. Hence, it's an API addition.

## Trivial fixes
* `findfirst(==(Int8(-1)), [0xff])` and similar findlast, findnext and findprev is no longer buggy, see #54578
* `findfirst([0x0ff], Int8[-1])` is similarly no longer buggy, see #54578
* `findnext(==('\xa6'), "æ", 1)` and `findprev(==('\xa6'), "æa", 2)` no longer incorrectly throws an error
* Using `Base.first_utf8_byte` instead of `x::AbstractChar % UInt8` is safer and faster
* Fixed incorrect use of `GC.@preserve`, where the pointer was taken before the preserve block.
* More of the optimised string methods now also apply to `SubString{String}`

CI failures unrelated."}],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":4,"created_at":"2024-05-25T15:24:30Z","updated_at":"2024-05-29T18:23:16Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","active_lock_reason":null,"draft":false,"pull_request":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/pulls/54579","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/54579","diff_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/54579.diff","patch_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/54579.patch","merged_at":null},"body":"# Various fixes to string searching

## Larger things that needs decisions
* The internal union `Base.ByteArray` has been deleted. Instead, the unions `DenseInt8` and `DenseUInt8` have been added. These more comprehensively cover the types that was meant, e.g. `Memory{UInt8}` was incorrectly not covered by the former. As stated in the TODO, the concept of a "memory backed dense byte array" is needed throughout Julia, so this ideally needs to be implemented as a single type and used throughout Base. The fix here is a decent temporary solution. See #53178 #54581
* The `findall` docstring between two arrays was incorrectly not attached to the method - now it is. **Note that this change _changes_ the documentation** since it includes a docstring that was previously missed. Hence, it's an API addition.

## Trivial fixes
* `findfirst(==(Int8(-1)), [0xff])` and similar findlast, findnext and findprev is no longer buggy, see #54578
* `findfirst([0x0ff], Int8[-1])` is similarly no longer buggy, see #54578
* `findnext(==('\xa6'), "æ", 1)` and `findprev(==('\xa6'), "æa", 2)` no longer incorrectly throws an error
* Using `Base.first_utf8_byte` instead of `x::AbstractChar % UInt8` is safer and faster
* Fixed incorrect use of `GC.@preserve`, where the pointer was taken before the preserve block.
* More of the optimised string methods now also apply to `SubString{String}`


Closes #54578","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/54579/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/54579/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/comments/2138013458","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/54579#issuecomment-2138013458","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/54579","id":2138013458,"node_id":"IC_kwDOABkWpM5_b38S","user":{"login":"nhz2","id":39104088,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjM5MTA0MDg4","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/39104088?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/nhz2","html_url":"https://github.com/nhz2","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nhz2/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nhz2/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nhz2/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nhz2/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nhz2/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nhz2/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nhz2/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nhz2/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nhz2/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2024-05-29T18:23:15Z","updated_at":"2024-05-29T18:23:15Z","author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","body":"This PR is missing tests for searching different possible `DenseArray`s. This can lead to the following strange behavior:
```julia
julia> a = @view(collect(0x00:0x0f)[begin:2:end])
8-element view(::Vector{UInt8}, 1:2:15) with eltype UInt8:
 0x00
 0x02
 0x04
 0x06
 0x08
 0x0a
 0x0c
 0x0e

julia> b = read(IOBuffer(a))
8-element Vector{UInt8}:
 0x00
 0x01
 0x02
 0x03
 0x04
 0x05
 0x06
 0x07
```

There is currently a check for `require_one_based_indexing`.
Should there be an additional check here?
https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/blob/4ecdc373d62ac3a28e4ee93a584ead080e8b6113/base/iobuffer.jl#L19-L23","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/54636/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/54636/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null}}

If you have a question please search or post to our Discourse site: https://discourse.julialang.org.

1. The output of `versioninfo()`

3. A minimal working example (MWE), also known as a minimum reproducible example

If you're experiencing a problem with a particular package, open an issue on that
package's repository instead.

Thanks for contributing to the Julia project!

Here is a MWE:
```julia
julia> s = IOBuffer()
IOBuffer(data=UInt8[...], readable=true, writable=true, seekable=true, append=false, size=0, maxsize=Inf, ptr=1, mark=-1)

julia> skip(s, typemax(Int64))
IOBuffer(data=UInt8[...], readable=true, writable=true, seekable=true, append=false, size=0, maxsize=Inf, ptr=-9223372036854775808, mark=-1)

julia> position(s)
9223372036854775807

julia> read(s, UInt8)

[807576] signal 11 (128): Segmentation fault
in expression starting at REPL[10]:1
getindex at ./essentials.jl:375 [inlined]
read at ./iobuffer.jl:240
unknown function (ip: 0x775177259d32)
...
```

This happens on nightly as well as 1.10.2, both installed using juliaup.

```julia
julia> versioninfo()
Julia Version 1.12.0-DEV.274
Commit a3f710e2a35 (2024-03-31 01:56 UTC)
Build Info:
  Official https://julialang.org/ release
Platform Info:
  OS: Linux (x86_64-linux-gnu)
```

 position(io::GenericIOBuffer) = io.ptr - io.offset - 1
 
 function skip(io::GenericIOBuffer, n::Integer)
-    seekto = io.ptr + n
-    n < 0 && return seek(io, seekto-1) # Does error checking
-    io.ptr = min(seekto, io.size+1)
-    return io
+    skip(io, clamp(n, Int))
+end
+function skip(io::GenericIOBuffer, n::Int)
+    if signbit(n)
+        seekto = clamp(widen(position(io)) + widen(n), Int)
+        seek(io, seekto) # Does error checking
+    else
+        @assert io.ptr ≤ io.size + 1","path":"base/iobuffer.jl","commit_id":"f1981e4b6a0cbf088661b6aa088973879027c52e","original_commit_id":"f1981e4b6a0cbf088661b6aa088973879027c52e","user":{"login":"nhz2","id":39104088,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjM5MTA0MDg4","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/39104088?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/nhz2","html_url":"https://github.com/nhz2","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nhz2/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nhz2/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nhz2/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nhz2/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nhz2/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nhz2/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nhz2/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nhz2/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nhz2/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"body":"It's ok for this assert to be removed.  position(io::GenericIOBuffer) = io.ptr - io.offset - 1
 
 function skip(io::GenericIOBuffer, n::Integer)
-    seekto = io.ptr + n
-    n < 0 && return seek(io, seekto-1) # Does error checking

+    skip(io, clamp(n, Int))
+end
+function skip(io::GenericIOBuffer, n::Int)
+    if signbit(n)
+        seekto = clamp(widen(position(io)) + widen(n), Int)
+        seek(io, seekto) # Does error checking
+    else

{"action":"created","comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/pulls/comments/1564801154","pull_request_review_id":1999690537,"id":1564801154,"node_id":"PRRC_kwDOABkWpM5dRPiC","diff_hunk":"@@ -260,22 +260,28 @@ bytesavailable(io::GenericIOBuffer) = io.size - io.ptr + 1
 position(io::GenericIOBuffer) = io.ptr - io.offset - 1
 
 function skip(io::GenericIOBuffer, n::Integer)
-    seekto = io.ptr + n
-    n < 0 && return seek(io, seekto-1) # Does error checking
-    io.ptr = min(seekto, io.size+1)
-    return io
+    skip(io, clamp(n, Int))
+end
+function skip(io::GenericIOBuffer, n::Int)
+    if signbit(n)
+        seekto = clamp(widen(position(io)) + widen(n), Int)
+        seek(io, seekto) # Does error checking
+    else
+        n_max = io.size + 1 - io.ptr
+        io.ptr += min(n, n_max)
+        io
+    end
 end
 
 function seek(io::GenericIOBuffer, n::Integer)
+    seek(io, clamp(n, Int))
+end
+function seek(io::GenericIOBuffer, n::Int)
     if !io.seekable
         ismarked(io) || throw(ArgumentError("seek failed, IOBuffer is not seekable and is not marked"))
         n == io.mark || throw(ArgumentError("seek failed, IOBuffer is not seekable and n != mark"))
     end
-    # TODO: REPL.jl relies on the fact that this does not throw (by seeking past the beginning or end

If you have a question please search or post to our Discourse site: https://discourse.julialang.org.

2. How you installed Julia
3. A minimal working example (MWE), also known as a minimum reproducible example

If you're experiencing a problem with a particular package, open an issue on that
package's repository instead.

Thanks for contributing to the Julia project!
-->
Here is a MWE:
```julia
julia> b = pushfirst!([0x02], 0x01)
2-element Vector{UInt8}:
 0x01
 0x02

julia> take!(IOBuffer(b))
5-element Vector{UInt8}:
 0x01
 0x02
 0x69
 0x73
 0x69
```

```julia
julia> versioninfo()
Julia Version 1.12.0-DEV.347
Commit c0611e8c49c (2024-04-15 17:22 UTC)
Build Info:
  Official https://julialang.org/ release
Platform Info:
  OS: Linux (x86_64-linux-gnu)
  CPU: 16 × AMD Ryzen 7 7800X3D 8-Core Processor
  WORD_SIZE: 64
  LLVM: libLLVM-16.0.6 (ORCJIT, znver4)
Threads: 1 default, 0 interactive, 1 GC (on 16 virtual cores)
```

I think the issue is
https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/blob/d8b9810b8908560cc4aaa744c0e587f4dfd11bfb/base/iobuffer.jl#L461
using `StringVector(io.size)` instead of `StringVector(nbytes)`.

The `pushfirst!` causes the `IOBuffer` to have a nonzero `offset` field (added in https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/53192) which makes `nbytes` and `io.size` different.

This has happened since #53896

Here is a MWE.
```
julia> a = Memory{UInt8}(undef, 10);

julia> fill!(a, 0x01);

julia> String(view(a, 1:2))
"\x01\x01"

julia> a
0-element Memory{UInt8}
```
I expected `a` to not be changed.

This is causing issues in https://github.com/JuliaWeb/HTTP.jl/issues/1166

```
julia> a = Memory{UInt8}(undef, 10);

"\x01\x01"

0-element Memory{UInt8}
```
I expected `a` to not be changed.

This is causing issues in https://github.com/JuliaWeb/HTTP.jl/issues/1166

Changes part of #53896

The main difference between `view` and `unsafe_vector` is that the result of `view` has a fixed size, and creates a copy when passed to `String`.

The `String(view(` pattern is fairly common: https://juliahub.com/ui/Search?q=String(view&type=code and as more packages start using `Memory` it may be hard to tell if `view` is being used safely or not.

I'm not sure about the name `unsafe_vector`, but the docstring says 

> It is only safe to
    resize `v` if `m` is subseqently not used.

So I added an `unsafe_` prefix.

Ref https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/issues/53552#issuecomment-2024288283 for a discussion on why `view` on a `Memory` should produce an ","user":{"login":"KnutAM","id":19473844,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjE5NDczODQ0","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/19473844?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/KnutAM","html_url":"https://github.com/KnutAM","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/KnutAM/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/KnutAM/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/KnutAM/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/KnutAM/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/KnutAM/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/KnutAM/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/KnutAM/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/KnutAM/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/KnutAM/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":4,"created_at":"2024-03-04T00:46:11Z","updated_at":"2024-03-04T20:26:51Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"When running `Pkg.add("CairoMakie")` in an empty project I'm getting this, `Error: "C:\\Users\\meyer\\.julia\\artifacts\\a8244d6d23cbb895fcd39dd3eddb859a0c05d1c6" could not be made`, which I seems to occur as the atomic rename fails after https://github.com/JuliaLang/Pkg.jl/pull/3768. 

However, before getting there, I hit https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/issues/34700, but that could be due to a failure related to above, but the `rm` is in a final block...

To "fix" the problem, I first hacked `Base.rm`
```julia
function Base.rm(path::String; force::Bool=false, recursive::Bool=false)
   if !(force && recursive)
       return invoke(Base.rm, Tuple{AbstractString}, path; force, recursive)
   else
       max_attempts = 3
       attempts = 0
       while attempts < max_attempts
           attempts += 1
           try
               invoke(Base.rm, Tuple{AbstractString}, path; recursive=true, force=true)
           catch err
               if isa(err, Base.IOError) && attempts < max_attempts
                   println("Trying again for \"", path, "\"")
                   attempts == (max_attempts - 1) && sleep(1.0)
                   continue
               else
                   println("Failed for \"", path, "\"")
                   rethrow(err)
               end
           end
       end
   end
end
```
(but this could just as well be done in `create_artifact`) 

And modified `_mv_temp_artifact_dir` (which I couldn't hack since the dispatch is concrete...)
```julia
function _mv_temp_artifact_dir(temp_dir::String, new_path::String)::Nothing
    if !isdir(new_path)
        # This next step is like
        # `mv(temp_dir, new_path)`.
        # However, `mv` defaults to `cp` if `rename` returns an error.
        # `cp` is not atomic, so avoid the potential of calling it.
        err = ccall(:jl_fs_rename, Int32, (Cstring, Cstring), temp_dir, new_path)
        # Ignore rename error, but ensure `new_path` exists.
        if !isdir(new_path)
            println("Just do cp 💀")
            mv(temp_dir, new_path)
        end
        if !isdir(new_path)
            error("$(repr(new_path)) could not be made")
        end
        chmod(new_path, filemode(dirname(new_path)))
        set_readonly(new_path)
    end
    nothing
end
```

`Just do cp 💀` is printed twice, along with a couple of `Trying again for ...`. 
In fact, it seems like the `x264_jll` and `Pixman_jll` are the ones that needs retries, not sure if by chance or anything special about these. 

Strangely, trying many times, it seemed to work, but fails again if I empty the artifacts folder...

It might be good to put the `rm` at `src\Artifacts.jl:376` in a try block. If the `rm` fails there will be some extra junk in the artifact directory, but no serious problems. I initially didn't do that because I thought `rm` failing was a very rare event.

If you run `Pkg.add("CairoMakie")` again after it fails it should hopefully make progress. Even if there is an error, the artifact folder might still be in a valid state.

However, before getting there, I hit https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/issues/34700, but that could be due to a failure related to above, but the `rm` is in a final block...

To "fix" the problem, I first hacked `Base.rm`
```julia
function Base.rm(path::String; force::Bool=false, recursive::Bool=false)
   if !(force && recursive)
       return invoke(Base.rm, Tuple{AbstractString}, path; force, recursive)
   else
       max_attempts = 3
       attempts = 0
       while attempts < max_attempts
           attempts += 1
           try
               invoke(Base.rm, Tuple{AbstractString}, path; recursive=true, force=true)
           catch err
               if isa(err, Base.IOError) && attempts < max_attempts
                   println("Trying again for \"", path, "\"")
                   attempts == (max_attempts - 1) && sleep(1.0)
                   continue
               else
                   println("Failed for \"", path, "\"")
                   rethrow(err)
               end
           end
       end
   end
end
```
(but this could just as well be done in `create_artifact`) 

And modified `_mv_temp_artifact_dir` (which I couldn't hack since the dispatch is concrete...)
```julia
function _mv_temp_artifact_dir(temp_dir::String, new_path::String)::Nothing
    if !isdir(new_path)
        # This next step is like
        # `mv(temp_dir, new_path)`.
        # However, `mv` defaults to `cp` if `rename` returns an error.
        # `cp` is not atomic, so avoid the potential of calling it.
        err = ccall(:jl_fs_rename, Int32, (Cstring, Cstring), temp_dir, new_path)
        # Ignore rename error, but ensure `new_path` exists.
        if !isdir(new_path)
            println("Just do cp 💀")
            mv(temp_dir, new_path)
        end
        if !isdir(new_path)
            error("$(repr(new_path)) could not be made")
        end
        chmod(new_path, filemode(dirname(new_path)))
        set_readonly(new_path)
    end
    nothing
end
```

`Just do cp 💀` is printed twice, along with a couple of `Trying again for ...`. 
In fact, it seems like the `x264_jll` and `Pixman_jll` are the ones that needs retries, not sure if by chance or anything special about these. 

Strangely, trying many times, it seemed to work, but fails again if I empty the artifacts folder...

However, before getting there, I hit https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/issues/34700, but that could be due to a failure related to above, but the `rm` is in a final block...

To "fix" the problem, I first hacked `Base.rm`
```julia
function Base.rm(path::String; force::Bool=false, recursive::Bool=false)
   if !(force && recursive)
       return invoke(Base.rm, Tuple{AbstractString}, path; force, recursive)
   else
       max_attempts = 3
       attempts = 0
       while attempts < max_attempts
           attempts += 1
           try
               invoke(Base.rm, Tuple{AbstractString}, path; recursive=true, force=true)
           catch err
               if isa(err, Base.IOError) && attempts < max_attempts
                   println("Trying again for \"", path, "\"")
                   attempts == (max_attempts - 1) && sleep(1.0)
                   continue
               else
                   println("Failed for \"", path, "\"")
                   rethrow(err)
               end
           end
       end
   end
end
```
(but this could just as well be done in `create_artifact`) 

And modified `_mv_temp_artifact_dir` (which I couldn't hack since the dispatch is concrete...)
```julia
function _mv_temp_artifact_dir(temp_dir::String, new_path::String)::Nothing
    if !isdir(new_path)
        # This next step is like
        # `mv(temp_dir, new_path)`.
        # However, `mv` defaults to `cp` if `rename` returns an error.
        # `cp` is not atomic, so avoid the potential of calling it.
        err = ccall(:jl_fs_rename, Int32, (Cstring, Cstring), temp_dir, new_path)
        # Ignore rename error, but ensure `new_path` exists.
        if !isdir(new_path)
            println("Just do cp 💀")
            mv(temp_dir, new_path)
        end
        if !isdir(new_path)
            error("$(repr(new_path)) could not be made")
        end
        chmod(new_path, filemode(dirname(new_path)))
        set_readonly(new_path)
    end
    nothing
end
```

`Just do cp 💀` is printed twice, along with a couple of `Trying again for ...`. 
In fact, it seems like the `x264_jll` and `Pixman_jll` are the ones that needs retries, not sure if by chance or anything special about these. 

Strangely, trying many times, it seemed to work, but fails again if I empty the artifacts folder...

However, before getting there, I hit https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/issues/34700, but that could be due to a failure related to above, but the `rm` is in a final block...

To "fix" the problem, I first hacked `Base.rm`
```julia
function Base.rm(path::String; force::Bool=false, recursive::Bool=false)
   if !(force && recursive)
       return invoke(Base.rm, Tuple{AbstractString}, path; force, recursive)
   else
       max_attempts = 3
       attempts = 0
       while attempts < max_attempts
           attempts += 1
           try
               invoke(Base.rm, Tuple{AbstractString}, path; recursive=true, force=true)
           catch err
               if isa(err, Base.IOError) && attempts < max_attempts
                   println("Trying again for \"", path, "\"")
                   attempts == (max_attempts - 1) && sleep(1.0)
                   continue
               else
                   println("Failed for \"", path, "\"")
                   rethrow(err)
               end
           end
       end
   end
end
```
(but this could just as well be done in `create_artifact`) 

And modified `_mv_temp_artifact_dir` (which I couldn't hack since the dispatch is concrete...)
```julia
function _mv_temp_artifact_dir(temp_dir::String, new_path::String)::Nothing
    if !isdir(new_path)
        # This next step is like
        # `mv(temp_dir, new_path)`.
        # However, `mv` defaults to `cp` if `rename` returns an error.
        # `cp` is not atomic, so avoid the potential of calling it.
        err = ccall(:jl_fs_rename, Int32, (Cstring, Cstring), temp_dir, new_path)
        # Ignore rename error, but ensure `new_path` exists.
        if !isdir(new_path)
            println("Just do cp 💀")
            mv(temp_dir, new_path)
        end
        if !isdir(new_path)
            error("$(repr(new_path)) could not be made")
        end
        chmod(new_path, filemode(dirname(new_path)))
        set_readonly(new_path)
    end
    nothing
end
```

`Just do cp 💀` is printed twice, along with a couple of `Trying again for ...`. 
In fact, it seems like the `x264_jll` and `Pixman_jll` are the ones that needs retries, not sure if by chance or anything special about these. 

Strangely, trying many times, it seemed to work, but fails again if I empty the artifacts folder...

PR should improve error messages if an anti-virus prevents the temporary directory the artifact is unpacked into from being renamed or deleted. Ref #3822 

The issue is that an error that occurs in a `finally` after a `try` and `catch` hides any error that occurred in the `try`. 

The following is the message I get when I simulate rm and rename failing on Linux with this PR:

to Nearest Fraction","user":{"login":"uniment","id":68589942,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjY4NTg5OTQy","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/68589942?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/uniment","html_url":"https://github.com/uniment","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/uniment/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/uniment/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/uniment/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/uniment/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/uniment/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/uniment/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/uniment/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/uniment/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/uniment/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[{"id":109356837,"node_id":"MDU6TGFiZWwxMDkzNTY4Mzc=","url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/labels/rationals","name":"rationals","color":"fbca04","default":false,"description":"The Rational type and values thereof"},{"id":909268321,"node_id":"MDU6TGFiZWw5MDkyNjgzMjE=","url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/labels/feature","name":"feature","color":"5664ff","default":false,"description":"Indicates new feature / enhancement requests"}],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":2,"created_at":"2024-02-02T03:19:39Z","updated_at":"2024-02-02T17:13:23Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"NONE","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"We often round numbers to the nearest fraction—for example, rounding to the nearest half or nearest quarter.

Normally we do something like `round(val*4)//4`, but maybe it would be good to incorporate this functionality into `round` itself? For my own use, I found these to be convenient:

```julia
Base.round(to::Rational, val::Real) = round(Int, val*to.den/to.num)*to
Base.floor(to::Rational, val::Real) = floor(Int, val*to.den/to.num)*to
Base.ceil(to::Rational,  val::Real) = ceil(Int,  val*to.den/to.num)*to
```

Example use:
```julia
julia> floor.(1//3, 0:1/6:1)
7-element Vector{Rational{Int64}}:
 0//1
 0//1
 1//3
 1//3
 2//3
 2//3
 1//1
```

In trying to perform
```
Rational{Int8}(-128) / Rational{Int8}(0)
```
we get an `OverflowError`
```
ERROR: OverflowError: checked arithmetic: cannot compute |x| for x = -128::Int8
Stacktrace:
 [1] checked_abs
   @ ./checked.jl:128 [inlined]
 [2] gcd(a::Int8, b::Int8)
   @ Base ./intfuncs.jl:52
 [3] divgcd
   @ ./rational.jl:44 [inlined]
 [4] //(x::Rational{Int8}, y::Rational{Int8})
   @ Base ./rational.jl:73
 [5] /(x::Rational{Int8}, y::Rational{Int8})
   @ Base ./rational.jl:360
 [6] top-level scope
   @ REPL[2]:1
```
within the implementation of division, instead of the expected `DivideError`. `checked_abs` is obviously not part of the division contract.



Julia installed from the dmg, versioninfo:
```
julia> versioninfo()
Julia Version 1.9.2
Commit e4ee485e909 (2023-07-05 09:39 UTC)
Platform Info:
  OS: macOS (arm64-apple-darwin22.4.0)
  CPU: 12 × Apple M2 Max
  WORD_SIZE: 64
  LIBM: libopenlibm
  LLVM: libLLVM-14.0.6 (ORCJIT, apple-m1)
  Threads: 1 on 8 virtual cores
```","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/51730/reactions","total_count":1,"+1":1,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/51730/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/comments/1937981901","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/issues/51730#issuecomment-1937981901","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/51730","id":1937981901,"node_id":"IC_kwDOABkWpM5zg0HN","user":{"login":"nhz2","id":39104088,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjM5MTA0MDg4","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/39104088?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/nhz2","html_url":"https://github.com/nhz2","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nhz2/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nhz2/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nhz2/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nhz2/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nhz2/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nhz2/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nhz2/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nhz2/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nhz2/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2024-02-12T02:01:14Z","updated_at":"2024-02-12T02:01:14Z","author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","body":"This can be closed now, it was fixed by #51944","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/comments/1937981901/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}

function foo!(c,a,b) 
    c .= copysign.(a, b)
    nothing
end
N = 1000

```

numbers"}],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":0,"created_at":"2024-02-22T15:28:46Z","updated_at":"2024-02-22T15:28:47Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"The current method for `//` on `Integer` and `Complex{<:Integer}` can overflow, silently giving an incorrect answer. For example:

```julia
julia> Int8(8) // Int8(100)im
0//1 - 50//1*im
```
The expected answer is `0//1 - 2//25*im`

The issue is caused by `abs2` and `conj` on `Complex{Int8}` silently overflowing in:
https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/blob/ccba6c91e24ebc0940acc2f8c390e6391ce0e292/base/rational.jl#L101

One solution could be to specialize this method on the many different combinations of `Complex{<:Rational}`, `Complex{<:Integer}`, `Rational`, `Integer`, and `Number` for the inputs, and check for overflow when doing any `Integer` math. Maybe there is a nicer way to fix this issue.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/53435/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/53435/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null}}

{"action":"opened","number":53453,"pull_request":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/pulls/53453","id":1741420942,"node_id":"PR_kwDOABkWpM5ny_mO","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/53453","diff_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/53453.diff","patch_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/53453.patch","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/53453","number":53453,"state":"open","locked":false,"title":"Avoid silent overflow in `/(x::Rational, y::Complex{Integer})`","user":{"login":"nhz2","id":39104088,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjM5MTA0MDg4","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/39104088?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/nhz2","html_url":"https://github.com/nhz2","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nhz2/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nhz2/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nhz2/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nhz2/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nhz2/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nhz2/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nhz2/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nhz2/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nhz2/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"body":"This change catches the overflow in 
```julia
(Int8(1)//Int8(1)) / (Int8(100) + Int8(100)im)
```
instead of incorrectly returning 
```julia
25//8 - 25//8*im
```

This PR changes the dispatch of `/(x::Rational, y::Complex{<:Union{Integer,Rational}})`
to default to a method in `complex.jl` that has better promotion rules and edge case checking.

This includes the checks added in #32050, fixing #23134 for rational numbers.

I also added an extra check to make sure the following still works.
```julia
iszero(zero(Rational{Int8}) / complex(Int8(100), Int8(100)))
```

The previous dispatch to `//` ignored overflow errors (see #53435), but this worked if the numerator also happened to be zero.

to default to a method in `complex.jl` that has better promotion rules and edge case checking.

This includes the checks added in #32050, fixing #23134 for rational numbers.

I also added an extra check to make sure the following still works.
```julia
iszero(zero(Rational{Int8}) / complex(Int8(100), Int8(100)))
```

The previous dispatch to `//` ignored overflow errors (see #53435), but this worked if the numerator also happened to be zero.

```julia
julia> function bar(a::R, z::S) where {R<:Real, S<:Complex}
           T = promote_type(R,S)
           z_p = T(z)
           if z_p isa Complex{<:Rational}
               # avoid overflow if a is zero and z isn't zero
               if iszero(z_p)
                   throw(DivideError())
               elseif iszero(a)
                   return a*inv(oneunit(z_p))
               end
           end
           a*inv(z_p)
       end
bar (generic function with 1 method)

{"action":"opened","number":53503,"pull_request":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/pulls/53503","id":1746755909,"node_id":"PR_kwDOABkWpM5oHWFF","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/53503","diff_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/53503.diff","patch_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/53503.patch","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/53503","number":53503,"state":"open","locked":false,"title":"make `Base.uabs` public 2","user":{"login":"nhz2","id":39104088,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjM5MTA0MDg4","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/39104088?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/nhz2","html_url":"https://github.com/nhz2","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nhz2/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nhz2/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nhz2/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nhz2/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nhz2/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nhz2/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nhz2/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nhz2/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nhz2/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"body":"`Base.uabs` is helpful when writing code that works with generic integers without worrying about silent overflows or overflow errors when using integers from [SaferIntegers.jl](https://github.com/JeffreySarnoff/SaferIntegers.jl)

`Base.uabs` is already being used by a few packages https://juliahub.com/ui/Search?q=uabs&type=code&w=true and has a docstring. However, it is currently an internal function.

the issue is not encountered on v1.9.3:
```
(jl_tdZRQD) pkg> add P4est_jll
   Resolving package versions...
  Downloaded artifact: P4est
  Downloaded artifact: P4est
ERROR: Unable to automatically download/install artifact 'P4est' from sources listed in 'C:\Users\Alex\.julia\packages\P4est_jll\kfjxs\Artifacts.toml'.
Sources attempted:
- https://pkg.julialang.org/artifact/89a337ea6f60a4fd58999ab73dea099e41032138
    Error: Tree Hash Mismatch!
  Expected git-tree-sha1:   89a337ea6f60a4fd58999ab73dea099e41032138
  Calculated git-tree-sha1: ed75b82e0dd9b53c4ac4e70376f3e6f330c72767
- https://github.com/JuliaBinaryWrappers/P4est_jll.jl/releases/download/P4est-v2.8.1+2/P4est.v2.8.1.x86_64-w64-mingw32-mpi+microsoftmpi.tar.gz
    Error: Tree Hash Mismatch!
  Expected git-tree-sha1:   89a337ea6f60a4fd58999ab73dea099e41032138
  Calculated git-tree-sha1: ed75b82e0dd9b53c4ac4e70376f3e6f330c72767
```

Troubleshooting on Slack with @sloede and @fredrikekre:
```
julia> using Tar, CodecZlib, Downloads, SHA

julia> artifact = Downloads.download("https://pkg.julialang.org/artifact/89a337ea6f60a4fd58999ab73dea099e41032138")
"C:\\Users\\Alex\\AppData\\Local\\Temp\\jl_glqlGnAL6b"

julia> shasum = bytes2hex(open(SHA.sha256, artifact))
"1abe2cd4e2c31d34c840d3042f94d6b34d85762c6be0fbd480eaf52136b566e1"

julia> open(GzipDecompressorStream, artifact) do io
           tree_hash = Tar.tree_hash(io)

       end

and

to inside the `download_artifact` function.

It also modifies `download_verify_unpack` to return a special symbol if it ends up changing symlinks.

#3643 using @staticfloat's approach in https://github.com/JuliaLang/Pkg.jl/issues/3643#issuecomment-1880111897

This PR moves the decision of whether to copy symlinks from https://github.com/JuliaIO/Tar.jl/blob/6269b5b8c4c863b1fe2beef53666273e3bbc021b/src/Tar.jl#L244-L246
and
https://github.com/JuliaLang/Pkg.jl/blob/3c86ba27e904807e13beb8cb0466ed70365b0b2d/src/PlatformEngines.jl#L385-L390
to inside the `download_artifact` function.

It also modifies `download_verify_unpack` to output which symlinks were not able to be created.

The error message added in #3745 is logged if there is a hash mismatch and symlinks were changed, but it is not an error, instead, the artifact is moved to the expected ```
(jl_tdZRQD) pkg> add P4est_jll
   Resolving package versions...
  Downloaded artifact: P4est
  Downloaded artifact: P4est
ERROR: Unable to automatically download/install artifact 'P4est' from sources listed in 'C:\Users\Alex\.julia\packages\P4est_jll\kfjxs\Artifacts.toml'.
Sources attempted:
- https://pkg.julialang.org/artifact/89a337ea6f60a4fd58999ab73dea099e41032138
    Error: Tree Hash Mismatch!
  Expected git-tree-sha1:   89a337ea6f60a4fd58999ab73dea099e41032138
  Calculated git-tree-sha1: ed75b82e0dd9b53c4ac4e70376f3e6f330c72767
- https://github.com/JuliaBinaryWrappers/P4est_jll.jl/releases/download/P4est-v2.8.1+2/P4est.v2.8.1.x86_64-w64-mingw32-mpi+microsoftmpi.tar.gz
    Error: Tree Hash Mismatch!
  Expected git-tree-sha1:   89a337ea6f60a4fd58999ab73dea099e41032138
  Calculated git-tree-sha1: ed75b82e0dd9b53c4ac4e70376f3e6f330c72767
```

Troubleshooting on Slack with @sloede and @fredrikekre:
```
julia> using Tar, CodecZlib, Downloads, SHA

julia> artifact = Downloads.download("https://pkg.julialang.org/artifact/89a337ea6f60a4fd58999ab73dea099e41032138")
"C:\\Users\\Alex\\AppData\\Local\\Temp\\jl_glqlGnAL6b"

julia> shasum = bytes2hex(open(SHA.sha256, artifact))
"1abe2cd4e2c31d34c840d3042f94d6b34d85762c6be0fbd480eaf52136b566e1"

julia> open(GzipDecompressorStream, artifact) do io
           tree_hash = Tar.tree_hash(io)
       end
"89a337ea6f60a4fd58999ab73dea099e41032138"

julia> open(GzipDecompressorStream, artifact) do io
           mktempdir() do tmp
               Tar.extract(io, tmp)
               tree_hash = bytes2hex(Pkg.GitTools.tree_hash(tmp))
           end
       end
"ed75b82e0dd9b53c4ac4e70376f3e6f330c72767"
```
